free_text: The disintegration of nitrogen by fast neutrons.
Energy resolution varies from 90 keV at 4.2 MeV
to 55 keV at 8.2 MeV.
The neutron sources which were used in the
experiments were Li7(p,n)Be7, T(p,n)He5 and the
D(d,n)He3 reactions.
The ionization chamber was filled with pure nitrogen
or a mixture of nitrogen and argon.
Experiments were carried out with a number of different
fillings of nitrogen and a nitrogen-argon mixture.
Grid-type ionization chamber
A long counter was used for monitoring
the neutron flux.
Data correction from F. Gabbard, 1964/3.
Translated from SCISRS
converted to REACTION formalism
BIB update, converted to lower case.
